failedAssertions.reason: Backend initialization failed - llama.cpp cannot convert HuggingFace model to GGUF. Returns 503 with proper error message. Endpoint structure correct but inference blocked by backend limitation.
Backend initialization failed - same root cause as VAL-API-002. Streaming endpoint exists, returns 503 with proper error message.
Backend initialization failed - same root cause. System messages accepted in request but inference blocked.
Backend initialization failed - same root cause. Temperature parameter accepted in request but inference blocked.
Backend initialization failed - same root cause. max_tokens parameter accepted in request but inference blocked.
Backend initialization failed - same root cause. Stop sequences parameter accepted in request but inference blocked.

Invalid JSON returns 422 (FastAPI/Pydantic validation error convention) instead of 400 as contract expects. API correctly rejects malformed JSON with descriptive error message. This is a convention difference, not functional failure.

appliedUpdates.description: VAL-API-012 marked as passed - invalid model now correctly returns 404 before backend initialization is attempted
VAL-API-013 updated with clearer issue description - 422 is FastAPI convention for JSON validation errors

testDetails.round3Focus: Re-tested 8 assertions that failed in Round 2 (VAL-API-002 through VAL-API-007, VAL-API-012, VAL-API-013)

VAL-API-012.notes: GET /v1/models/nonexistent-model-12345 correctly returns HTTP 404 with error message 'Model not found' before backend initialization is triggered

VAL-API-013.notes: POST /v1/chat/completions with invalid JSON returns HTTP 422 (FastAPI/Pydantic validation error) instead of 400. Error includes detailed JSON decode error location.

VAL-API-002 through VAL-API-007.notes: All chat completion endpoints return HTTP 503 with proper error structure. The endpoints correctly parse and validate request parameters (system messages, temperature, max_tokens, stop sequences), but inference fails due to backend GGUF conversion limitation.

issuesIdentified.description: llama.cpp backend cannot convert HuggingFace models (sshleifer/tiny-gpt2) to GGUF format. The backend searches for pre-converted GGUF variants but none exist for small test models. Conversion tools are not available in the environment.
issuesIdentified.suggestedFix: Either: (1) Add automatic GGUF conversion capability, (2) Use a model with pre-converted GGUF variants (e.g., models from TheBloke), (3) Implement a mock backend for testing

issuesIdentified.description: FastAPI/Pydantic returns 422 for JSON validation errors by convention, but contract expects 400. This is not a functional failure - the API correctly rejects invalid input with descriptive errors.
issuesIdentified.suggestedFix: Either update contract to accept 422 for validation errors, or add custom exception handler to return 400
